caption:
Stop writing reduce() to group arrays.

Object.groupBy() is built into JavaScript now. One line. No lodash.

Which pattern do you use? A or B?

#javascript #es2025 #webdev #coding #programming #developer #arrays #groupby #cleancode #codingtips

slides.title: Which grouping is cleaner?
slides.problem: Group array items by a property

a.label: Manual reduce

b.label: Object.groupBy()

slides.title: B wins. One line. Built-in.

verdicts.title: 8 lines for a common pattern
verdicts.text: Every JS project has this exact reduce somewhere. Verbose, error-prone, and everyone writes it slightly differently.

verdicts.title: Native. No dependencies.
verdicts.text: Object.groupBy takes an array and a key function. Returns a null-prototype object. All browsers since 2024.

slides.takeaway: The most copy-pasted reduce pattern is now a built-in.

slides.title: It works with any key logic.
slides.sub: Strings, computed values, even Map.groupBy for non-string keys.

items.text: Object.groupBy(array, keyFn) — group by string key
Map.groupBy(array, keyFn) — group by any key

slides.teaser: Next: Web Workers — heavy computation off the main thread
